text: A light source is introduced through a ten-millimeter port in the right iliac fossa, with the light cable wrapped around the rectum. The abdomen is desufflated while the light remains in place, allowing it to be switched on and off as needed.
At the beginning of the transanal part of the procedure, the light source is visible through the rectal tissue. The surgeon initiates lumen closure using a purse string suture technique.
The light source is visible throughout the transanal dissection, providing enhanced transillumination that facilitates safe dissection by clearly illuminating the tissue planes.
